The Cloud Operations team provides 24x7x365 support for all Syniti SaaS & Hosting customers globally.  This business unit is responsible for the day-to-day management and support of the cloud operations environment including the uptime, performance and high availability of all customer supporting systems inside of the SaaS & Hosted environments.  The SaaS & hosted ecosystem is comprised of multi-tiered applications, microservice architectures, containers & virtual servers as well as large & complex multi-terabyte SQL database systems.  The Cloud Operations environment exists across both private cloud and public cloud hyperscalers such as AWS. The ROLE The Senior Cloud Operations Engineer is an application lifecycle management & support resource who is experienced in Cloud Operations or managed service delivery environments. The individual is responsible for the daily oversight and support of Cloud Operations systems and applications. The Senior Cloud Operations Engineer is responsible for handling incidents and requests flowing through the Syniti customer ticketing portal and resolving issues within the defined SLAs. WHAT YOU WILL DO Take ownership of the administration, optimization, and scaling of virtual servers (Windows and Linux), containers, and container orchestration systems (Kubernetes, Docker), ensuring high availability and resilience in multi-cloud/hyperscaler environments like AWS. Diagnose, analyze, and resolve cloud infrastructure-related issues, including network failures, compute performance degradation, storage bottlenecks, and security vulnerabilities, ensuring minimal service disruptions. Oversee all cloud operations service delivery activities, including designing and managing system deployments, upgrades, patching strategies, and decommissioning processes for optimal efficiency and minimal downtime. Identify, propose, and implement strategic improvements in operational processes, enhancing efficiency through automation, process refinement, and best practices adoption. Serve as the escalation point for complex incidents, lead root cause analysis (RCA) efforts, and drive permanent fixes to prevent recurring issues. Provide guidance and mentorship to junior engineers, fostering a culture of learning and innovation within the Cloud Operations team. Zilliz is a fast-growing startup developing the industry’s leading vector database company for enterprise-grade AI. Founded by the engineers behind Milvus, the world’s most popular open-source vector database, the company builds next-generation database technologies to help organizations quickly create AI applications. On a mission to democratize AI, Zilliz is committed to simplifying data management for AI applications and making vector databases accessible to every organization.


Zilliz is looking for an early-career engineer to join our team as an Open Source Support Engineer and grow into a technical expert while helping developers build applications with Milvus, the leading open-source vector database.

Milvus powers vector search for thousands of developers worldwide. By helping open-source users succeed in real production environments, this role directly contributes to the growth of the Milvus ecosystem, while providing critical feedback that shapes the product’s evolution.

This is an excellent opportunity for recent graduates or engineers early in their careers who are excited to solve real infrastructure problems, work directly with users, and develop deep expertise in distributed systems and vector search technology.
